ogTitle: Boambee East vs Boat Harbour: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Boambee East and Boat Harbour.
heading: Boambee East vs Boat Harbour — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Boambee East, New South Wales and Boat Harbour, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Boat Harbour has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Boambee East (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Boambee East or Boat Harbour safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Boambee East has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Boat Harbour scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Boambee East scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Boat Harbour scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Boambee East or Boat Harbour?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Boat Harbour has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
